text:
There once was a wise old owl that could blend into the woods like mist. He was loved by all the small creatures for his gentle wisdom. One day, a clever squirrel named Sparky boasted that he could fly anywhere.

"Why don't you fly up to the moon? It's an extraordinary place!" Sparky chirped.

Old Owl pondered this boldly adventurous idea.

"I think you'll find the moon is quite distant," he said wisely.

"Yes!" declared Sparky. "But I could fly forever!"

The squirrels giggled.

"First," Owl continued, "you fly across the ocean. That's quite a big gap."

"Oh, I fly through the sky," Sparky laughed. "That's nothing!"

"Hmm," Owl scratched his chin thoughtfully. "Next, you'd have to cross continents."

"Pfft," Sparky interrupted. "That's easy!"

"And last," Owl declared melodically, "you'd struggle to find the way back."

"Well," Sparky
